qa_pairs.question: Why are some software packages only available at specific sites?
qa_pairs.answer: Some software packages are only available at specific sites due to license restrictions on the package in question.

qa_pairs.question: What chemistry software is available on the Nibi cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Nibi cluster, the following chemistry software is available: ADF (modules adf/2016.106, adf/2017.207, adf/2018.104, adf/2019.305), AMS (module ams/2020.102), AMBER (module amber/16), and Gaussian (modules gaussian/g03.d01, gaussian/g09.e01, gaussian/g16.b01, gaussian/g16.c01).

qa_pairs.question: What is ADF and which cluster is it available on?
qa_pairs.answer: ADF, the Amsterdam Density Functional Modeling Suite, is a computational chemistry software available on the Nibi cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Which versions of ADF are installed?
qa_pairs.answer: Installed versions of ADF include adf/2016.106, adf/2017.207, adf/2018.104, and adf/2019.305.

qa_pairs.question: What is the AMS software package?
qa_pairs.answer: AMS (Amsterdam Modeling Suite products) is a chemistry software package.

qa_pairs.question: Which cluster hosts the AMS software?
qa_pairs.answer: The AMS software is available on the Nibi cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the AMBER software?
qa_pairs.answer: AMBER is the Amber Molecular Dynamics Package, a chemistry software.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for ADF, AMS, and AMBER?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation for ADF can be found via [[ADF]], for AMS via [[AMS]], and for AMBER via [[AMBER]].

qa_pairs.question: What is DIRAC and which cluster is it available on?
qa_pairs.answer: The DIRAC program computes molecular properties using relativistic quantum chemical methods and is available on the Cedar cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What versions of DIRAC are available on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: DIRAC versions 16.0 and 17.0 are available on the Cedar cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is Galaxy used for?
qa_pairs.answer: Galaxy is a scientific workflow, data integration, and data and analysis persistence and publishing platform designed to make computational biology accessible to research scientists without programming or systems administration experience.

qa_pairs.question: How can a group set up a Galaxy instance on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: Any group on Cedar can have one Galaxy instance, which will be run under a shared account created by admins. Users should contact technical support to set it up.

qa_pairs.question: What is the homepage for Galaxy?
qa_pairs.answer: The homepage for Galaxy is https://usegalaxy.org/.

qa_pairs.question: What is Gaussian software?
qa_pairs.answer: Gaussian is a general-purpose computational chemistry software package.

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters have Gaussian software installed?
qa_pairs.answer: Gaussian software is installed on both the Nibi and Fir clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What versions of Gaussian are available on the Nibi cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Nibi cluster, Gaussian versions g03.d01, g09.e01, g16.b01, and g16.c01 are available.

qa_pairs.question: What versions of Gaussian are available on the Fir cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Fir cluster, Gaussian versions g03.d01, g09.b01, g09.e01, g16.a03, g16.b01, and g16.c01 are available.

qa_pairs.question: What is GBrowse and where is it available?
qa_pairs.answer: GBrowse is a combination of database and interactive web pages for manipulating and displaying annotations on genomes, available on the Cedar cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is the homepage for GBrowse?
qa_pairs.answer: The homepage for GBrowse is http://gmod.org/wiki/GBrowse.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to use SAS software on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: SAS on Cedar is licensed software available to users from the Alberta School of Business who are eligible to run SAS.

qa_pairs.question: What is SAS software used for?
qa_pairs.answer: SAS is a software suite developed by SAS Institute for advanced analytics, multivariate analyses, business intelligence, data management, and predictive analytics.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Trans-Proteomic Pipeline (TPP)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Trans-Proteomic Pipeline (TPP) is a collection of integrated tools for MS/MS proteomics, developed at the SPC.

qa_pairs.question: Can I get a web interface for TPP on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: On Cedar, a TPP web interface (tpp_gui) can be provided per group upon user request.

qa_pairs.question: What type of software is Galaxy?
qa_pairs.answer: Galaxy is a biology (bio) software.

qa_pairs.question: What is the module name for SAS software?
qa_pairs.answer: The module name for SAS software is sas/9.4.
